journey.name: Timatic Simulator — autocheck endpoints
journey.description: End-to-end journey covering all three IATA AutoCheck simulator endpoints: document check (with and without US ESTA advisory), APIS check, and realtime gate MRZ check. Negative steps verify that missing or wrong Authorization headers return 401. Positive steps send Authorization: Bearer timatic-test-2026 — the Timatic:ApiToken Azure App Setting on the simulator must be set to timatic-test-2026 for positive steps to pass.

steps.name: Document check — LHR→JFK (ESTA)
steps.description: POST a document check for a British passport holder travelling LHR→JFK. The simulator inspects the final arrival airport (JFK) and appends an ESTA advisory because JFK is a US gateway. All other requirement flags (visaRequired, healthDocRequired, transitVisaRequired) are false.

apiCall.note: Authorization header must match the Timatic:ApiToken app setting

advisories.description: ESTA registration required prior to travel.

expected.description: 200 OK — document check accepted. passportRequired is true. visaRequired, healthDocRequired, and transitVisaRequired are all false. Because JFK is a recognised US gateway, the advisories array contains exactly one ESTA advisory with mandatory: true. transactionIdentifier echoes the request value.
expected.validationNotes: advisories array must contain exactly one item with type ESTA and mandatory true. requirements array must be empty. dataAsOf is today at 08:00 UTC.

steps.name: Document check — LHR→CDG (no advisory)
steps.description: POST a document check for a British passport holder travelling LHR→CDG. CDG is not a US gateway so no advisory is appended. Confirms the simulator only adds the ESTA advisory for US destinations.

expected.description: 200 OK — document check accepted. CDG is not a US gateway so advisories array is empty. All requirement flags are false.
expected.validationNotes: advisories must be an empty array. requirements must be an empty array.

steps.name: APIS check — valid check-in data
steps.description: POST APIS (Advance Passenger Information) for a British passport holder on AX001 LHR→JFK. Simulates the check-in submission pathway. The simulator always returns apisStatus: ACCEPTED with fineRisk: LOW and a generated audit reference in the format TMC-YYYY-MM-DD-XXXXXXXX.

expected.description: 200 OK — APIS accepted. apisStatus is ACCEPTED, carrierLiabilityConfirmed is true, fineRisk is LOW, warnings is empty. auditRef follows the format TMC-YYYY-MM-DD-XXXXXXXX.
expected.validationNotes: auditRef format: TMC- + date + - + 8 uppercase hex characters. warnings must be empty. transactionIdentifier echoes the request value.

steps.name: Realtime gate check — valid MRZ
steps.description: POST a realtime gate check with a valid ICAO 9303 TD3 Machine Readable Passport MRZ for John Smith (GBR, DOB 1980-01-01, expiry 2031-01-01). The simulator parses the two MRZ lines and returns decision: GO with the extracted document fields in parsedDocument.

expected.description: 200 OK — gate check returns GO. conditionsMet and carrierLiabilityConfirmed are true. parsedDocument contains fields extracted from the MRZ: surname SMITH, givenNames JOHN, nationality GBR, dateOfBirth 1980-01-01, documentExpiryDate 2031-01-01, documentNumber 123456789. auditRef follows the format TMC-YYYY-MM-DD-GATE-XXXXXX.
expected.validationNotes: parsedDocument.surname and parsedDocument.givenNames are extracted from MRZ line 1 using the double-chevron (<<) delimiter. MRZ dates YYMMDD: years >= 60 → 1900s, years < 60 → 2000s.

steps.name: Document check — no Authorization header (401)
steps.description: POST a document check without an Authorization header. The simulator rejects all requests that lack a Bearer token before reaching handler logic.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Authorization header supplied. The BearerTokenValidator returns false immediately when the header is absent. No request body processing occurs.
expected.validationNotes: Response body is empty. The simulator returns 401 without a JSON error envelope for auth failures.

steps.name: APIS check — wrong bearer token (401)
steps.description: POST an APIS check with a bearer token that does not match the configured Timatic:ApiToken. The SHA-256 hash comparison fails and the simulator returns 401 without processing the body.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — bearer token is present but the SHA-256 hash of 'this-is-the-wrong-token' does not match the hash of the configured Timatic:ApiToken. The simulator rejects the request before parsing the body.
expected.validationNotes: Response body is empty. Hash comparison is constant-time to prevent timing-based token discovery.

steps.name: Realtime check — malformed body (400)
steps.description: POST a realtime check with a valid Authorization header but a malformed JSON body. The simulator returns 400 Bad Request when the body cannot be deserialized.

apiCall.note: Body is intentionally null to trigger 400

expected.description: 400 Bad Request — valid token but null/empty request body. The simulator deserializes to null and returns 400 without executing any check logic.
expected.validationNotes: Response body is empty. The 400 is returned for both unparseable JSON and a successfully deserialized null body.
